body.title: /sprint-batch — run multiple tickets in dependency order
body.result: ~/.claude/skills/sprint-batch/SKILL.md — selectors: today-slate, slate:planned, slate:ad-hoc, decision:D-..., tag:..., explicit ids. Topo-sorts by deps, shared setup+teardown, one commit per ticket, includes the 'oh and I also fixed this' retroactive-ticket pattern. 5dfd5c34.
body.description:
Multi-ticket sprint with one shared setup (git pull, venv, env) instead of per-ticket.

## Selectors
- `/sprint-batch today-slate` — all open slate items
- `/sprint-batch slate:planned` / `slate:ad-hoc`
- `/sprint-batch decision:D###` — everything tied to a specific decision
- `/sprint-batch tag:<tag>` — e.g. `tag:WorkflowOverhaul`
- `/sprint-batch T-x T-y T-z` — explicit list

## Dependency ordering
Filter out gated tickets. Topo-sort by explicit related_to edges. If cycles: bail with a clear message and let Akien pick.

## Per-ticket loop
Shared setup ONCE up front; then per ticket: claim → build → test → cleanup → doc-refresh → commit+push → close. Shared teardown (savestateauto) at end.

## Abort/skip
If a ticket fails mid-batch, prompt: abort remaining? skip this one and continue? re-plan?

## Not in scope
- /fixit rewrite — separate ticket.
